children.code:
def CalculateTotal(items: list, product_details: list) -> float:
    total = 0.0
    for item, product in zip(items, product_details):
        quantity = item.get('quantity', 0)
        price = product.get('price', 0.0)
        total += quantity * price
    return total

subprd.purpose: Order Management System
subprd.description:
INPUT FORMAT:
  Format: json
  Description: Input is a JSON object with command and order_data fields, passed to manage_order function.
  Schema:
    command: string (one of: create_order, pay_order, ship_order, complete_order, cancel_order, list_orders, get_user_orders, list_products)
    order_data: object containing relevant fields: user_id (int), order_id (int), items (list of {product_id, quantity}), status_filter (string), user_filter (int), low_stock (bool)
  Examples:
    {"command": "create_order", "order_data": {"user_id": 1, "items": [{"product_id": 2, "quantity": 2}]}}
    {"command": "pay_order", "order_data": {"order_id": 1}}
    {"command": "list_products", "order_data": {"low_stock": true}}

OUTPUT FORMAT:
  Format: json
  Description: Output is a JSON object with success, message, and data fields.
  Schema:
    success: boolean
    message: string describing result
    data: object containing relevant data (e.g., order_id, total_price, new_balance, orders list, products list)
  Examples:
    {"success": true, "message": "\u8ba2\u5355\u521b\u5efa\u6210\u529f", "data": {"order_id": 1, "total_price": 199.8}}
    {"success": false, "message": "\u5546\u54c1\u5e93\u5b58\u4e0d\u8db3", "data": {"product_id": 1, "available": 10, "requested": 15}}

Functional Requirements:
[FR-001] Create Order: User creates an order by specifying user ID and list of items. System checks user existence, product stock, calculates total price, deducts stock, and creates order record with status pending.
[FR-002] Pay Order: User pays for an order. System checks order exists and status is pending, checks user balance sufficient, deducts balance, updates order status to paid.
[FR-003] Ship Order: Ship an order. Checks order exists and status is paid, updates status to shipped.
[FR-004] Complete Order: Complete an order. Checks order exists and status is shipped, updates status to completed.
[FR-005] Cancel Order: Cancel an order. Checks order exists and status is pending or paid. Restores product stock, refunds user balance if paid, updates status to cancelled.
[FR-006] List Orders: List orders with optional filters by user and status. Displays order details including items, total price, status, and creation time.
[FR-007] Get User Orders: Get all orders for a specific user. Calculates total spent and counts of orders by status.
[FR-008] List Products: List all products with info and current stock. Optionally filter low stock products.

constraints.description: Storage: memory - Three independent in-memory data sources: users, products, orders

acceptance_criteria.description: Create order successfully when user exists and stock sufficient
Create order fails with appropriate error when user does not exist
Create order fails with appropriate error when stock insufficient
Pay order succeeds when order is pending and balance sufficient
Pay order fails when order not pending
Pay order fails when balance insufficient
Ship order succeeds when order is paid
Complete order succeeds when order is shipped
Cancel order succeeds when order is pending or paid, restores stock and refunds balance if paid
Cancel order fails when order is shipped or completed
List orders returns correct orders with filters
Get user orders returns correct statistics
List products returns all products, low stock filter works

fix_summary.suggestion: If repair_action is redecompose, add/rewrite child boundaries so every child input has a source and parent does not access resources directly.
